description: Andrew joined HM Treasury (HMT) in September 2022 as Director Public Spending, where he leads the teams responsible for delivering high quality government financial reporting and data, with a focus on using this information to improve decision making and accountability across government. He is also Deputy Head of the Government Finance Function setting the Strategy and Standards for the function.  Andrew is a CIMA qualified accountant. He has 27 years’ experience in the Civil Service across several departments including DWP, HMRC and DEFRA. Andrew lives in Gateshead, is married to Elizabeth and has two teenage children (Lucas and Hayden). He is a big football fan (supporting Newcastle United) and a keen runner.

details.body:
Andrew joined HM Treasury (HMT) in September 2022 as Director Public Spending, where he leads the teams responsible for delivering high quality government financial reporting and data, with a focus on using this information to improve decision making and accountability across government. He is also Deputy Head of the Government Finance Function setting the Strategy and Standards for the function.  Andrew is a CIMA qualified accountant.

He has 27 years’ experience in the Civil Service across several departments including DWP, HMRC and DEFRA.

Andrew lives in Gateshead, is married to Elizabeth and has two teenage children (Lucas and Hayden). He is a big football fan (supporting Newcastle United) and a keen runner.

Andrew joined HM Treasury (HMT) in September 2022 as Director Public Spending, where he leads the teams responsible for delivering high quality government financial reporting and data, with a focus on using this information to improve decision making and accountability across government. He is also Deputy Head of the Government Finance Function setting the Strategy and Standards for the function.  Andrew is a CIMA qualified accountant.

He has 27 years’ experience in the Civil Service across several departments including DWP, HMRC and DEFRA.

Andrew lives in Gateshead, is married to Elizabeth and has two teenage children (Lucas and Hayden). He is a big football fan (supporting Newcastle United) and a keen runner.
The GIAA Board brings together the Executive Management Team and non-executive board members.

Andrew joined HM Treasury (HMT) in September 2022 as Director Public Spending, where he leads the teams responsible for delivering high quality government financial reporting and data, with a focus on using this information to improve decision making and accountability across government. He is also Deputy Head of the Government Finance Function setting the Strategy and Standards for the function.  Andrew is a CIMA qualified accountant.

He has 27 years’ experience in the Civil Service across several departments including DWP, HMRC and DEFRA.

Andrew lives in Gateshead, is married to Elizabeth and has two teenage children (Lucas and Hayden). He is a big football fan (supporting Newcastle United) and a keen runner.

The Deputy Head of the Government Finance Function leads on the governance and strategy of the Government Finance Function, investing in people, sharing best-practice and developing frameworks, guidance and tools to support more than 10,000 people working in finance across government.

role.title: Deputy Head of the Government Finance Function

role_appointments.title: Andrew Cartner - Deputy Head of the Government Finance Function

title: Andrew Cartner
